title: Avenella flexuosa
intro: Avenella flexuosa, also known as flexible oat grass or bent, is a perennial grass species belonging to the genus Avenella. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Avenella flexuosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Avenella flexuosa is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into supplements and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal medicine.
taxonomy: Avenella flexuosa is a species of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. It is a type of grass that belongs to the genus Avenella, which is part of the family Poaceae. This classification is further supported by the APG IV system, which places Avenella flexuosa in the clade of monocots.
